beliefs.gloss: Visual learning enables abstraction/inference without language.
beliefs.evidence: Our framework, termed Vision ARC (VARC), achieves 60.4% accuracy... Abstraction and inference can arise directly from visual learning.

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial: see Fig. 7(b)(c).

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: Extending from 1D positional embedding to its 2D counterpart is beneficial: see Fig. 7(b)(c).

beliefs.gloss: Explicit 2D positional modeling preserves image structure.
beliefs.evidence: we replace the 2D ROPE... with a 1D ROPE and observe a degradation of 3.5 points.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas patchification enriches data, reduces overfitting, encourages spatial priors.
beliefs.evidence: the 2×2 patchification leads to a noticeable gain... each patch can cover multiple colors... which substantially enriches the data space for learning.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas formulation enables augmentations for translation/scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: we apply fully flexible translation augmentation on the canvas... this setting yields an additional gain of 2.9 points.

beliefs.gloss: Augmentations encourage learning mappings invariant to geometric transformations.
beliefs.evidence: These data augmentations encourage the model to learn underlying mappings invariant to geometric transformations... yields an additional gain.

beliefs.gloss: Canvas formulation enables augmentations for translation/scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: we further apply the scale augmentation enabled by the concept of canvas. Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: Augmentations encourage learning mappings invariant to geometric transformations.
beliefs.evidence: These data augmentations encourage the model to learn underlying mappings invariant to geometric transformations... Scale augmentation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: ViT lacks inductive bias for scale invariance.
beliefs.evidence: the ViT architecture has little to no inductive bias about scale invariance. This can explain why scale augmentation yields a substantial gain.

beliefs.gloss: Visual common sense is learned from offline training data.
beliefs.evidence: offline training greatly improves the performance of TTT, suggesting that common sense about the visual world can be learned.

beliefs.gloss: Some tasks are solvable from scratch (tabula rasa).
beliefs.evidence: even without offline training, our TTT strategy can achieve nontrivial accuracy... suggesting that some tasks... can be solved tabula rasa.

beliefs.gloss: Larger models beyond current regime lead to overfitting.
beliefs.evidence: Going beyond this regime can lead to overfitting in our current setting, as shown in Tab. 1 for the 66M ViT model.

beliefs.gloss: Majority voting consolidates predictions from different views.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Majority voting consolidates predictions from different views.
beliefs.evidence: multi-view inference further boosts to 49.8, thanks to majority voting.

beliefs.gloss: Single pixel error invalidates entire ARC prediction.
beliefs.evidence: in ARC, a mistake on even a single pixel renders the entire prediction incorrect. This may explain the large gain.

beliefs.gloss: Multi-view inference cost is negligible compared to TTT.
beliefs.evidence: As the multi-view inference cost is negligible compared with test-time training cost, it is virtually nearly free.

beliefs.gloss: Attention maps reveal pixel-to-pixel reasoning relationships.
beliefs.evidence: These attention maps show that our model can correctly reason about the relationship between a source pixel and its target pixel.

beliefs.gloss: Early layer attention reflects pattern core influence.
beliefs.evidence: some layers exhibit strong attention to the 3 × 3 neighborhood, reflecting the influence of the pattern's core.

beliefs.gloss: Later layer attention reflects rule for extending pixels.
beliefs.evidence: some other layers (e.g., layers 7-9) focus on the outward-radiating rays, corresponding to the rule that extends colored pixels.

beliefs.gloss: Task embedding visualization suggests learning inter-task relations.
beliefs.evidence: This visualization suggests that our method attempts to learn the relations between different tasks, which is an essential ability.

beliefs.gloss: Correct predictions exist in views but are outvoted.
beliefs.evidence: This result indicates that our model produces correct predictions in some of the many views, although such correct cases are not sufficiently populated.

beliefs.gloss: Model handles ambiguity by proposing multiple rule interpretations.
beliefs.evidence: our method uncovers different solutions that are plausible.

beliefs.gloss: Some ARC tasks admit multiple plausible rules.
beliefs.evidence: the rule can be interpreted as either 'going-through-only' or 'touching'.
